tasks.title: Review frontend/backend directory layout at scale
tasks.description: Confirm the flat top-level frontend/ and backend/ layout still fits as the project grows (recipe_management, shared types/utilities, etc.). Revisit if a different structure (e.g. shared/ package, monorepo tooling) becomes necessary.

tasks.title: Review port exposure and networking for production
tasks.description: Local dev exposes frontend:4200, backend:8000, mysql:3306, and adminer:8080 directly on the host. For production, put the backend behind a reverse proxy with TLS, don't expose the MySQL port publicly, and reconsider whether Adminer should run at all outside local dev.

tasks.title: Replace PHP built-in dev server with a production-grade setup
tasks.description: backend/Dockerfile currently runs `php -S` (PHP's built-in server), which is fine for local dev but lacks proper concurrency/process management. Replace with PHP-FPM + nginx (or similar) before any real deployment.

tasks.title: Move DB credentials out of docker-compose.yml
tasks.description: MySQL root/user passwords are currently hardcoded directly in docker-compose.yml for local dev convenience. Before production, source these from a .env file (excluded from version control) or a proper secrets manager, and use strong generated passwords rather than 'root'/'eve'.

tasks.title: Confirm no dependency on the Windows/Git-Bash Docker workaround
tasks.description: Local dev commands on Windows need MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1 prefixed to docker run/docker compose, or Git Bash mangles container paths like /app into Windows paths. Verify the production build/deploy pipeline (likely Linux-based CI) doesn't depend on this workaround; document it as a Windows-dev-only note if it's still relevant there.

tasks.title: Use multi-stage Docker builds to slim production images
tasks.description: backend/Dockerfile installs unzip and git via apt-get solely so Composer can run; these build-time tools shouldn't ship in the final image. frontend/Dockerfile runs the Angular dev server (`ng serve`) with the full node_modules and dev toolchain rather than a production build. Restructure both Dockerfiles as multi-stage builds: build/install in one stage, copy only the built artifacts (compiled PHP app without dev deps; Angular's `ng build` output served via a lightweight web server like nginx) into a slim final image.
